_meta.israeli_context: Israel MOH prescribes route alongside T/C category. Vaporizer recommended over combustion since 2017 Israeli MOH clinical guidance.

routes.mechanism: Cannabinoids/terpenes volatilize below combustion temperature (160-230°C); inhaled directly into alveolar capillaries; bypasses first-pass metabolism

pharmacokinetics.variability: High — depends on inhalation technique, temperature, device, lung capacity

advantages: Fastest onset — critical for breakthrough pain, acute nausea, panic attacks
Precise real-time titration — patient can stop when effect achieved
Terpene preservation at correct temperature (vs combustion which destroys most terpenes)
No combustion byproducts (vs smoking: >100 toxins from pyrolysis)
Discrete — minimal odor vs smoking
Short duration useful when effect not needed all day
disadvantages: Shorter duration than oral — requires redosing
Variable bioavailability — inconsistent dosing
Device cost (€80-500+ for quality vaporizer)
Requires charging/maintenance
Lung irritation possible (less than smoking but still present in COPD patients)
Not suitable for: pediatric, respiratory disease, patients who cannot inhale properly

optimal_temperatures.160_165: Light effects; primarily terpene activation (caryophyllene, pinene); minimal THC/CBD
optimal_temperatures.170_180: Therapeutic window — THC/CBD optimal activation; myrcene, limonene, linalool active
optimal_temperatures.180_190: Deeper effect; more CBN; linalool, nerolidol active; sedating
optimal_temperatures.190_plus: Risk of combustion at 230°C; degradation products above 210°C

device_types.conduction: Most affordable; heats chamber directly; hot spots; slightly more combustion risk
device_types.convection: Heated air passes through material; superior terpene preservation; recommended for medical use
device_types.cartridge_oil: Concentrated extract in pod; consistent dosing; terpenes may be added artificially; convenient
device_types.desktop: Most precise temperature control (e.g., Volcano); ideal for medical sessions

routes.israeli_context: Israeli MOH guidelines (2017+) recommend vaporization as preferred inhalation method over smoking. Israeli pharmacies provide device guidance. Volcano vaporizer common recommendation.

routes.mechanism: Same as vaporization but cannabis burned at 600-900°C; cannabinoids survive partially; massive terpene destruction; generates >100 toxins from plant matter combustion

pharmacokinetics.bioavailability_thc_pct: 2-56 (highly variable; joint vs pipe vs bong)

advantages: Fastest method
No device required
Culturally familiar to many patients
disadvantages: Combustion produces CO, benzene, formaldehyde, acrolein, tar — all harmful to respiratory mucosa
Destroys majority of terpenes (loses entourage effect)
Most variable dosing of all methods
Lung/bronchial damage with chronic use
Strong odor
Israeli MOH specifically discourages smoking as administration route
routes.recommendation: NOT RECOMMENDED for medical use. Vaporization superior in every therapeutic metric.
routes.israeli_context: Israeli MOH discourages smoking; licenses specify 'inhalation' route which should be via vaporizer. Some patients still smoke — important to educate on risk difference.

routes.mechanism: Oil placed under tongue or on gum mucosa; cannabinoids absorbed directly into sublingual/buccal capillary network; partial first-pass bypass; remainder swallowed and absorbed via GI

pharmacokinetics.variability: Moderate — affected by holding time under tongue, lipid medium, individual mucosal absorption

advantages: Longer duration than inhalation — better for maintenance dosing
Predictable dosing with calibrated droppers (mg precision)
Faster than oral capsule (partial sublingual bypass)
Discrete — no odor
No respiratory irritation
Suitable for pediatric (special formulations), elderly, respiratory patients
Easier for caregivers to administer
Multiple T/C ratios available as oils
disadvantages: Slower onset than inhalation — not for acute symptoms
Taste can be strong/unpleasant (terpene-rich oils)
CBD bioavailability improved with fatty food (not always practical when sublingual)
First-pass metabolism reduces THC effectiveness vs inhalation (but metabolite 11-OH-THC is MORE psychoactive than THC itself — oral THC can be unexpectedly strong)
Duration variability — some patients absorb much faster/slower

dosing_guidance.start_low: 0.5-1 ml (check product mg/ml) once daily evening
dosing_guidance.titration: Increase by 0.25ml every 3-5 days until therapeutic effect
dosing_guidance.target: CBD-dominant: 25-100mg CBD/day typical; THC oils: 5-15mg THC typical starting (2.5mg elderly)
dosing_guidance.timing_note: Evening dosing for THC (sedating); CBD can be morning or split morning-evening

administration_tips: Hold under tongue 60-90 seconds before swallowing for best sublingual absorption
Take with or after food for CBD (improves bioavailability up to 4x — fatty food best)
Calibrated dropper = consistent mg dosing — key advantage over inhalation
Do NOT mix with hot beverages (degrades cannabinoids)
Store in cool, dark location (terpene and cannabinoid degradation with heat/light)
routes.israeli_context: Most common prescription format in Israel 2023. T/C oils in 10ml or 30ml bottles with calibrated dropper. Israeli pharmacies: BOL Pharma, Tikun Olam, Intercure oils most prescribed.

routes.mechanism: Swallowed; absorbed in small intestine; full first-pass liver metabolism; THC converted to 11-OH-THC (highly psychoactive, longer half-life); delayed onset

pharmacokinetics.variability: Very high — gastric emptying rate, fed/fasted state, individual CYP450 variation; CBD increased 4-5x with high-fat meal

advantages: Longest duration of all routes — ideal for all-day coverage
Most discrete delivery
Precise mg dosing with capsules
No respiratory system involved
Consistent schedule possible (BID dosing)
disadvantages: Slowest onset — completely unsuitable for acute/breakthrough symptoms
11-OH-THC metabolite is more potent and longer-lasting than inhaled THC — risk of unexpected overdose particularly in edibles
Very high food-effect variability
Duration too long for short therapeutic windows
Difficult to titrate — 'wait and see' required
routes.caution_he: אכילה/כמוסות — לא מתאים להתחלה. 11-OH-THC חזק מ-THC הרגיל. מינון נמוך כפול ממה שחושבים. 'אני לא מרגיש — אאכל עוד' = שגיאה קלאסית → מנת יתר בשעה 2.

routes.mechanism: Cannabis-infused cream, gel, balm, or transdermal patch applied to skin; local absorption; minimal systemic absorption unless transdermal patch formulation; local CB1/CB2 receptors in skin

pharmacokinetics.bioavailability_systemic_pct: <1 (topical cream); up to 45 (transdermal patch with penetration enhancers)

advantages: No psychoactivity (standard topical) — safe for driving, work, children
Local anti-inflammatory, analgesic for arthritis, muscle pain, neuropathic skin pain
Targeted delivery without systemic effects
Transdermal patches: systemic delivery without smoking — useful for dysphagic patients
disadvantages: Limited systemic effect for most topicals
Transdermal patches expensive, limited availability in Israel
Difficult to standardize dosing
Not for acute internal symptoms (pain, nausea)
routes.israeli_availability: Topical CBD creams available OTC; THC topicals via prescription only; transdermal patches limited availability in Israel

routes.mechanism: Cannabinoids absorbed via rectal mucosa directly into hemorrhoidal veins → bypasses liver first-pass (significantly more than oral); CBD and THC well-absorbed rectally

pharmacokinetics.bioavailability_thc_pct: up to 80 (no first-pass = equivalent to IV)

advantages: High bioavailability — similar to IV
For patients who cannot swallow or inhale (cancer, severe GI disease, pediatric)
Minimal psychoactivity relative to high bioavailability (reasons unclear)
Excellent for IBD — direct rectal delivery for distal IBD
disadvantages: Patient compliance poor
Limited availability in Israel
Formulation challenges (must have proper excipients)
indications_primary: pediatric epilepsy when oral not possible
routes.israeli_availability: Limited; some compounding pharmacies. Gaining recognition in pediatric epilepsy protocols.

route_comparison_matrix.note: Quick selection guide by clinical priority

scenarios.second: inhalation_vaporizer (low dose, if oil not fast enough)

elderly.start_dose_note: Start at 25% of standard adult dose; CYP450 changes with age; polypharmacy risk high; fall risk with THC sedation

pediatric.note: Any pediatric use requires pediatric neurologist/specialist approval; primarily for refractory epilepsy and autism spectrum (research protocol)

safe_routes: oil_sublingual CBD-dominant (T0-T3/C15+) — wait 4h minimum before driving
not_safe: any THC-dominant inhalation or oil for at least 4-6 hours post-use
driving_patients.israeli_law: Israeli law: THC detection in blood = DUI regardless of time. CBD-only: generally not tested. Legal guidance: no THC products and driving same day.

oncology.note: Often requires combination: oil daily + vaporizer PRN for breakthrough; adjust based on opioid co-medication
